config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan সমাজ atau mayoritas, karena di situlah perlindungan terhadap penyalahgunaan kuasa paling penting. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan membatasi hak individu seminimal mungkin dan tetap adil bagi semua orang. Kalau masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa batas, suara kelompok kecil dan orang yang lemah mudah diabaikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that kind of blanket rule makes me wary because it can easily become a license for the majority or the government to trample unpopular people, especially when they are least able to defend themselves. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order do matter, but any limits should be narrow, justified, and subject to strong oversight. In practice, I would put the burden on society to prove why a rights restriction is necessary, not on individuals to prove they deserve protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu kalau dijadikan aturan umum, karena di Indonesia sendiri saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu; justru hak individu perlu dilindungi supaya pemerintah dan mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Yang masuk akal adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya: pembatasan hak hanya boleh dilakukan bila benar-benar perlu, jelas alasannya, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan sosial” bisa berubah jadi pembenaran untuk ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should automatically outrank the rights of the individual. From a U.S. perspective, I am very skeptical of that idea because history shows how quickly “for the public good” can become a cover for discrimination, censorship, or abuse of power against unpopular people. I do think society can limit individual rights in narrow cases like public safety, but those limits need a high bar, clear justification, and real oversight. If the statement means society always comes first, I disagree strongly, because that gives too much power to the majority and too little protection to the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk menekan yang lemah, dari minoritas sampai warga biasa yang tidak punya akses kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh jadi kartu bebas untuk mengorbankan hak dasar; pembatasan hanya sah kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan sosial mudah berubah jadi alat pembenaran ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, that sounds too close to giving the majority or the government a blank check, and history shows how easily “the common good” can be used to justify censorship, discrimination, or abuse. I do think there are limited cases where rights must be constrained for public safety or basic order, but those exceptions should be narrow, evidence-based, and heavily checked. If we lose the presumption in favor of individual rights, it becomes much easier to silence unpopular people and much harder to protect anyone when society gets it wrong.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
